description: Full-public compound profiles whose contraindications_or_flags field was reviewed and intentionally remains empty or non-prose because reliable evidence does not support a specific human contraindication. Safety notes retain supported findings and evidence limits.

exceptions.reason: Human evidence concerns Boswellia extract exposure, not constituent-specific contraindications.
One narrow human trial did not establish population- or disease-specific contraindications.
Human evidence concerns an AKBA-enriched extract, not isolated-compound contraindications.
Human safety is unknown; preclinical toxicology cannot define a human contraindication.
Systematic toxicity evidence is preclinical and cannot establish human organ-specific contraindications.
Limited human studies reported mild or no events but did not establish a categorical contraindication.
Evidence is primarily preclinical and does not define human contraindications.
Evidence is primarily preclinical and does not define human contraindications.
Evidence is primarily preclinical and does not define human contraindications.
The review found clinical studies are needed and did not establish a human contraindication.
A systematic review did not establish a categorical contraindication; interaction evidence was limited.
The regulatory assessment did not establish a disease-specific contraindication for supplemental betaine.
The regulatory assessment did not establish a disease-specific contraindication for supplemental betaine.
Small gastric-pH studies did not establish categorical disease contraindications.
Reliable product-specific human contraindication evidence was not identified; component evidence cannot be combined.
Short product-specific trials did not establish a categorical contraindication.
The human study used a multi-herb product and cannot establish catuaba-specific contraindications.
Small short-term product-specific trials did not establish a categorical contraindication.
The human trial used a diosgenin-rich yam extract, not isolated diosgenin.
No single safety profile can be assigned without an exact ingredient list and doses.
A regulatory concern involved a multi-constituent Cistanche extract and cannot establish an isolated-echinacoside contraindication.
Safety depends on the exact electrolytes, amounts, and use context; no fixed formulation is specified.
Erinacine evidence is preclinical; human safety evidence concerns whole Hericium preparations.
Human trials are lacking and preclinical findings cannot establish contraindications.
Human pregnancy evidence concerns oral ginger preparations, not isolated gingerol.
Human pregnancy evidence concerns oral ginger preparations, not isolated gingerols.
Human hemostasis evidence concerns standardized whole Ginkgo extract, not isolated ginkgolide B.
Human hemostasis evidence concerns standardized whole Ginkgo extract, not isolated ginkgolides.
The page represents an unspecified sleep formulation; isolated glycine evidence cannot define the product's contraindications.
Human adverse-event evidence concerns standardized guggul extract, not isolated guggulsterone contraindications.
A human systematic review did not establish categorical contraindications and safety reporting was limited.
A short formulation-specific trial did not establish a categorical liver or other contraindication.
The page represents an unspecified sleep formulation; isolated inositol evidence cannot define the product's contraindications.
Available human studies did not establish a categorical contraindication and preparations vary.
Human safety evidence concerns whole maca preparations, not isolated macamides.
Identified human evidence is topical and cannot establish oral contraindications.
A critical human review did not establish a categorical contraindication and interaction reporting was limited.
No single safety profile can be assigned without an exact ingredient list and doses.
Short isolated-taurine trials did not establish a categorical contraindication; multi-ingredient products require their own review.
The regulatory assessment did not establish a disease-specific contraindication for supplemental betaine.
Short product-specific trials and reviews did not establish a categorical contraindication.
